observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) for each type of livestock and require the designated General Services Officer to prepare the corresponding stock cards and reconcile the same to establish the correct balances and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted. Furthermore, request/apply for relief from Accountability on the dead/missing animals in accordance with COA Memorandum No. 92-751, Documentation on Petition/Requests for Relief from Accountability.
observations.reason: No records/ documents on file. Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor conduct an investigation on the allegations contained in the affidavit made by the borrowers on their payment of SEA loans. We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to all borrowers.
observations.reason: Not properly endorsed by the previous administration. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 40 to 42, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the daily remittance of collectors and likewise, monitor the issuance of accountable forms to collecting officers, by preparing the Individual Report/s of Collections and Deposits and the Consolidated Report of Collections and Deposits.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Treasurer who conducted the cash examination instructed the Municipal Treasurer to complete the missing ORs before submitting to COA for validation. No subsequent action has been taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to conduct an investigation and file appropriate charges against the accountable officers for the alteration of documents in violation of Article 171 of the Revised Penal Code, if warranted.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Mayor conducted an investigation; no subsequent action has been taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to produce the missing accountable forms or submit proper accounting thereof.
observations.reason: The former Audit Team instructed the Municipal Treasurer to complete the missing ORs before submitting to COA for validation. No subsequent action has been taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Designated GSO to prepare and complete the prescribed RPCPPE and submit
to the Audit Team;
• Municipal Accountant and the Designated GSO to prepare and maintain
complete and updated PPELC and property cards, respectively; and
• Municipal Accountant and the Designated GSO to reconcile their records
regularly

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to collect from the identified debtors and/or
gather all the required documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005
and submit the request for write-off to the Commission on Audit; and
• Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in
order to present the Loans Receivable-Others and Other Receivable accounts in
the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• confirm with the payees/suppliers/contractors and request them to submit
complete documents pertaining to their claims with a period within which to
respond shall be indicated in the letter-request for confirmation;
• in the event that the payees/suppliers/contractors failed to support their claims
within such given period, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of
the GF of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
allot time for the preparation of the reconciliation either on the face of the SCBAA
or disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided in Paragraphs 21
and 47 of IPSAS 1 and 24, respectively, to achieve transparency in financial
reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• justify the certification that all documents are complete, despite the absence of
the signature/certification of the Municipal Budget Office on the DV; and
• ensure that disbursements or disposition of government funds or property shall
invariably bear the approval of the proper officials, in compliance with the
provisions of Section 4(5 and 6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 138 of GAAM,
Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the In Charge of Office (ICO), of General Services Office to submit the Report on Physical Count of PPE, prepare the property cards and coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the PPE balance with the PPELC, and observe the provisions of Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume I on the prescribed timeline in the submission of RPCPPE to allow the necessary verification and reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Designated GSO submitted the RPCPPE. The report lacks necessary information and data such as unit of value, on hand per count and shortage/overage and remarks. Moreover, no property cards and PPE ledger cards were prepared. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 39-40, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: The report lacks necessary information and data such as unit of value, on hand per count and shortage/overage and remarks. Moreover, no property cards and PPE ledger cards were prepared. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 39-40, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the timely approval of the annual appropriation ordinance in compliance with Section 319 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to ensure that targeted project/program/activity under the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality for the year are attained for the benefit of the constituents in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.reason: Timely approval of the annual appropriation has been observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • provide the Barangays necessary support in enjoining the residents; • strictly enforce and intensify the campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents; • develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of the Municipality; and • strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Barangays already established their own MRF. No-littering policy has been enforced.
observations.reason: The Municipality fully implemented some recommendations (Barangays established MRF and enforced No-littering policy). The sanitary landfill was developed but still waiting for the approval of Environment Compliance Certificate; still waiting for the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of LCPC.
observations.reason: One percent of IRA was allocated for PPAs of LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit the RPT and SET shares due to the Provincial Government and the component barangay governments within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.reason: The RPT and SET shares are remitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.
observations.reason: All paid DVs of the Municipality were stamped “PAID”.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-390 and Republic Act 656.
observations.reason: On-going application for insurance of insurable properties. The Municipality already insured various properties with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to support the compliance with the requirement of COA Circular No. 92-382 and to ensure proper accounting of time deposits.
observations.managementAction: Closed Decision of the Office of the Ombudsman (OMBL-C-18-0020) ruling that there is compliance with Section 21 COA Circular No. 92-382. The Municipal Team has complied with the requirement.
observations.reason: Decision of the Office of the Ombudsman ruling that there is substantial compliance with the requirement of COA Circular No. 92-382.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to submit the listing of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are for implementation during the year, as well as ensure that appropriate signboards and/or public notices are posted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.reason: The MPDC and the Municipal Engineer already submitted the required listing of projects and signboards; information/signboards posted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the Local School Board to see to it that SEF is utilized for the purpose provided under Section 1 of RA No. 5447 and Sections 100 (c) and 272 of RA No. 7160 and other related rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The recommendation has been complied with. SEF utilized for the prescribed purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the prompt submission of contracts and its supporting documents for timely review.
observations.managementAction: The BAC already submitted contracts and its supporting documents within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The contracts and supporting documents were submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: • Bids and Awards Committee and the designated General Services Officer to procure commonly used supplies and equipment from DBM-PS in conformity with the government’s policy for transparency in government transactions and efficiency in procurement processes; and • Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the APP-CSE to DBM pursuant to DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-16.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already procured commonly used supplies and equipment from DBM-PS. The Municipal Budget Officer prepared and submitted the APP-CSE to DBM.
observations.reason: The Management already complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal point to regularly submit the Municipality’s approved GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed and strictly adhere to the above cited regulations.
observations.reason: The Municipality submitted the approved GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized balance of DRRMF for the year 2010 and 2011 totaling P3,601,759.16 pursuant to the above-cited regulations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant already reverted P3,601,759.16 of the LDRRMF balances as required.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO already submitted the required reports.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO submitted the required monthly reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to justify the non-posting of the notices of awards in the PhilGEPS for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects totaling P5,420,603.78 and to strictly adhere to the transparency provision cited above.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The BAC justified the non-posting and subsequently complied with the transparency provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balances totaling P524,347.51 from Other Payables to Due to BIR, Due to NGAs, Due to GOCCs, Due to LGUs and Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-003.
observations.reason: Total of P524,347.51 has already been reclassified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to conduct regular general revision of assessment to update current and previous taxable parcels for valuation and tax purposes in compliance with Section 219 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and allocating funds for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: General revision was already conducted at the Provincial Government level.
observations.reason: General revision of assessment was conducted through the Provincial Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Collectors to turn over daily and intact the collections to the Municipal Treasurer, and the latter to deposit the same promptly and intact to the depository bank.
observations.reason: The Management complied with the recommendation; Collectors turned over collections daily and Treasurer deposited them promptly.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the In-Charge of Office (ICO), General Services Office to submit the Report on Physical Count of PPE, prepare the property cards and coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the PPE balance with the PPELC, and observe the provisions of Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume I on the prescribed timeline in the submission of RPCPPE to allow the necessary verification and reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Management Letter issued to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal point to regularly submit the Municipality’s approved GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed and strictly adhere to the above cited regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Management Letter issued to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Management Letter issued to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
20. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 instruct the ICO-Municipal Accountant for the immediate submission
of the required financial reports and documents for Calendar Years
2017 and 2018;
 initiate immediate actions to employ and/or designate a full-fledge
Municipal Accountant; and
 ensure that the succeeding reports and documents are submitted
within the prescribed period pursuant to existing rules and
regulations.

27. We reiterate our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
 OIC – GSO to prioritize the submission of the inventory report;
 ICO - Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger
Cards and the OIC - GSO to prepare and maintain complete and
updated property cards and require them to reconcile the records
regularly.

33. We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal
Person to submit the Accomplishment Report and require the ICO-Municipal
Accountant to submit the pertinent disbursement vouchers for GAD Fund in
compliance with existing regulations to enable the Audit Team to conduct audit of the
funds, programs and activities on gender and development.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) for each type of livestock and require the designated General Services Officer to prepare the corresponding stock cards and reconcile the same to establish the correct balances and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted. Furthermore, request/apply for relief from Accountability on the dead/missing animals in accordance with COA Memorandum No. 92-751, Documentation on Petition/Requests for Relief from Accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the timely approval of the annual appropriation ordinance in compliance with Section 319 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to ensure that targeted project/program/activity under the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality for the year are attained for the benefit of the constituents in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: provide the necessary support in enjoining the remaining barangays to establish their own MRF and to strictly implement waste segregation at source; strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents; develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of the Municipality; and strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of LCPC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit the RPT and SET shares due to the Provincial Government and the component Barangays within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and Republic Act 656.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to support the investment of the idle funds of P16,891,738.13 in time deposit or HYSA in compliance with Section 21 COA Circular No. 92-382 backed up by specific sources of the funds used for the investment to establish whether these are essentially idle funds in accordance with the said Circular.
observations.managementAction: The former Municipal Accountant submitted the required authority on the additional deposit of 3,000,000.00 from the Sangguniang Bayan on June 26, 2009 has not yet submitted.
observations.reason: The required authority from the Sangguniang Bayan on the additional deposit of 3,000,000.00 has not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to submit the listing of all-on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are for implementation during the year, as well as information that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices have been already posted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The required documents were already submitted.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of reports due to demographic constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the Local School Board to see to it that SEF is utilized for the purpose provided under Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447, Sections 100 (c) and 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and other rules and regulations, relating to the defined usage or utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the prompt submission of contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely evaluation and verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: Various contracts and supporting documents were promptly submitted.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of some contracts were due to demographic constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: Bids and Awards Committee and the designated General Services Officer to procure commonly used supplies and equipment from the DBM-PS in conformity with the above cited Administrative Order and DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 to support the government’s policy for transparency in government transactions and efficiency in procurement processes; and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the APP-CSE to DBM pursuant to DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-16.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized balance of DRRMF for the year 2010 and 2011 totaling P3,601,759.16 pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the daily remittance of collectors and likewise, monitor the issuance of accountable forms to collecting officers, by preparing the Individual Report/s of Collections and Deposits and the Consolidated Report of Collections and Deposits.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to conduct an investigation and file appropriate charges against the accountable officers for the alterations of documents in violation of the provision of Article 171 of the Revised Penal Code, if warranted.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the daily remittance of collections from the Collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to deposit promptly and intact the collections turned over to him.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to produce the missing accountable forms or submit proper accounting thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal point to regularly submit the Municipality’s approved GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed and strictly adhere to the above cited regulations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Management Letter issued to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Management Letter issued to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the DVs, payrolls, official receipts and
financial reports in conformity with the above-cited regulations.

19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to submit immediately the financial statements and supporting schedules as of
December 31, 2017 and explain in writing their failure to submit the required financial
reports without prejudice to the filing of disciplinary action such as administrative
sanctions and suspension of salary pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445.

25. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• OIC – GSO to prioritize the submission of the inventory report;
• ICO - Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and
the OIC - GSO to prepare and maintain complete and updated property
cards and require them to reconcile the records regularly.

30. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to
submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report for GAD Fund in compliance
with COA Circular No. 2014-001 to facilitate evaluation of the success of the GAD
projects or programs.

35. We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report for DRRM Funds
in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009 to enable the Audit Team to evaluate
utilization of MDRRM funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the timely approval of the annual appropriation ordinance in compliance with Section 319 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to ensure that targeted project/program/activity under the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality for the year are attained for the benefit of the constituents in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of LCPC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit the RPT and SET shares due to the Provincial Government and the component Barangays within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and Republic Act 656.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to support the investment of the idle funds of P16,891,738.13 in time deposit or HYSA in compliance with Section 21 COA Circular No. 92-382 backed up by specific sources of the funds used for the investment to establish whether these are essentially idle funds in accordance with the said Circular.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to submit the listing of all-on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are for implementation during the year, as well as information that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices have been already posted in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the Local School Board to see to it that SEF is utilized for the purpose provided under Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447, Sections 100 (c) and 272 of Republic Act No. 7160 and other rules and regulations, relating to the defined usage or utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the prompt submission of contracts and its supporting documents, to ensure timely evaluation and verification thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Bids and Awards Committee and the designated General Services Officer to procure commonly used supplies and equipment from the DBM-PS in conformity with the above cited Administrative Order and DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 to support the government’s policy for transparency in government transactions and efficiency in procurement processes; and
Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the APP-CSE to DBM pursuant to DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-16.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal point to regularly submit the Municipality’s approved GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed and strictly adhere to the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal 
Accountant to revert the unutilized balance of DRRMF for the year 2010 and 2011 totaling P3,601,759.16 pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate an Acting Supply Officer or General Services Officer to attend to the property management in order to solve the issue.

Also, we recommended to require the Accounting Office to update the 	PPELC.
observations.managementAction: Updated the PPELC.
General Services Officer has been designated. No Reports were prepared. Discussed under observation no. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Accountable Officer to liquidate immediately his cash advance; 
instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letter to the accountable officer concerned; and
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to effect salary deduction in case of failure of the accountable officer to liquidate his outstanding cash advance.
observations.managementAction: Accountable Officer liquidated his cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to justify the non-posting of the notices of awards in the PhilGEPS for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects totaling P5,420,603.78 and strictly adhere to the transparency provision cited above.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Awards for the procurement of goods and infrastructures are now being posted in the PhilGEPS.
observations.reason: Various notice of awards were not posted to the PhilGEPS due to slow internet connection in the area.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to notify the payees or holder of checks issued one month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale so that concerned payees will be aware of the depository bank’s action on these issued checks six months after the date of its issuance.

We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts whenever stale checks are discovered, particularly during the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction:
All payees are notified. No more staled checks.



Prepared the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the actual amounts presented in the financial statements; and disclose the said reconciliation on the face of the SCBAA or in the notes to financial statements in compliance with Section 47 of the PPSAS No. 47, to enable the Municipality to better discharge its accountability obligations.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted the reconciliation of SCBAA and financial statements. Corresponding notes to financial statement were presented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balances of trust liabilities totaling P524,347.51 from Other Payables to Due to BIR, Due to NGAs, Due to GOCCs, Due to LGUs and Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-003 dated April 15, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Total of P355,327.85 has already been reclassified.
